text.title: Continuous Delivery Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the cd-basics methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every commit on the mainline branch is in a deployable state at all times.
rule.rationale: Without this invariant, 'release' becomes a manual testing campaign; CD is impossible.

rule.statement: New user-visible behaviour ships behind a feature flag defaulted OFF; flips are independent of deploys.
rule.rationale: Flags decouple deploy risk from release risk; rollback is a flag flip, not a redeploy.

rule.statement: DB migrations use expand-contract phases (add nullable → backfill → drop old). No destructive change in the same commit as code that depends on it.
rule.rationale: Atomic destructive migrations break the rollback path; expand-contract keeps every commit deployable.

rule.statement: Environments are defined in IaC (Terraform/Pulumi/CDK). Manual UI clicks in cloud consoles are forbidden for shared environments.
rule.rationale: Manual environment drift makes CD pipelines lie; IaC is the only way to keep staging ≡ prod.

rule.statement: Pipeline emits DORA events: deployment_frequency, lead_time_for_changes, change_failure_rate, mean_time_to_restore.
rule.rationale: Without DORA, you cannot prove CD adoption is improving anything.

rule.statement: Production deploy step requires a manual trigger (button press), but every prior stage is automated.
rule.rationale: Manual gate at prod keeps humans in the loop without blocking pipeline throughput.

rule.statement: If there is no CI, no tests, or compliance requires manual gates per release, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: CD requires prerequisites; adopting without them produces a broken pipeline.
